name: Abdomen CT With Contrast
description: Contrast-enhanced CT of the abdomen for evaluation of hepatic, biliary, and upper abdominal pathology

findings.technique: Contrast-enhanced CT of the abdomen was performed with portal venous phase imaging from the diaphragm through the pelvis.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Liver
sections.content: The liver demonstrates [normal attenuation / no mass / no lesion / homogeneous enhancement]. The hepatic vasculature is [patent / normal]. No biliary ductal dilation.

sections.name: Gallbladder
sections.content: The gallbladder is [present / not distended / no stones / no wall thickening]. No pericholecystic fluid or adenomyomatosis.

sections.name: Biliary Ducts
sections.content: The intrahepatic and extrahepatic bile ducts are [not dilated / normal caliber]. No stricture or filling defect.

sections.name: Spleen
sections.content: The spleen is [normal in size / not enlarged / no mass / no laceration]. No intrasplenic lesion.

sections.name: Pancreas
sections.content: The pancreas demonstrates [normal morphology / no mass / no pancreatitis / no ductal dilation]. The pancreatic duct is [not dilated / normal].

sections.name: Kidneys
sections.content: The bilateral kidneys demonstrate [normal cortical attenuation / no mass / no stone / no hydronephrosis]. No renal lesion.

sections.name: Adrenal Glands
sections.content: The bilateral adrenal glands are [normal in size / no mass / no hyperplasia]. No adrenal lesion.

sections.name: Aorta and IVC
sections.content: The abdominal aorta is [normal in caliber / no aneurysm / no dissection]. The inferior vena cava is [patent / normal].

sections.name: Bowel
sections.content: The stomach, duodenum, and small bowel demonstrate [no mass / no obstruction / no bowel wall thickening]. No pneumoperitoneum.

sections.name: Pelvic Organs
sections.content: The pelvic organs including the bladder, uterus/ovaries, and prostate are [unremarkable / no mass]. No abnormal enhancement.

impression:
1. Normal contrast-enhanced CT abdomen.
2. No mass, cyst, or inflammatory process identified in the upper abdomen.
author: RadReport Templates
